title: 1989 Equatorial Guinean presidential election
summary:
Presidential elections were held in Equatorial Guinea on 25 June 1989, the first since 1968, although incumbent Teodoro Obiang Nguema Mbasogo had his seven-year term starting in 1982 approved in a referendum in 1982. He was the only candidate and was re-elected unopposed with 99% of the vote.


== References ==
text: Presidential elections were held in Equatorial Guinea on 25 June 1989, the first since 1968, although incumbent Teodoro Obiang Nguema Mbasogo had his seven-year term starting in 1982 approved in a referendum in 1982. He was the only candidate and was re-elected unopposed with 99% of the vote.
